---
title: Theory of Holdouts
date: 2026-05-29
code-fold: false
echo: false
fig-cap-location: top
categories: []
number-sections: false
draft: true
cache: false
---

We need to define what counterfactual outcomes we want to contrast.

One of them is very clear -- it represents the state of the product had no further experiments been performed and no changes made.  It is, in essence, a version of the product frozen in time.  This is the "control" experience and serves the role of $Y(0)$ in standard potential outcomes notation.

The tricky part is $Y(1)$, and this is where a lot of approaches differ:
